Donation Guidelines

What does it mean to donate?

As BigFatPervs, we are aware of how we live in a society that is based on consumption, with money being it’s currency. The more money you have the less you can choose to contribute to society financially. In a consumer-focused space people come to an event (often) having paid for the event in advance. They do their thing within the parameters of the event, pay for their drinks and leave at a designated time expecting others to clean up after them.

In a DIY/contributor space it is less straight forward. You come to an event knowing that the minimum donation asked for by a DIY space is not an amount that is based on making profit. It’s not even based on covering costs. The donation amount is based on an amount the organizers feel is a fair minimum to ask of people with less to no income. Even then, people who cannot afford the donation are asked to give transparency in this, not to be judged on why they can’t afford it, but to acknowledge that there are other ways to contribute than only on a financial level.

Instead, as a participant, you are aware of your own financial capabilities and strive to donate more than a minimum donation when possible. Think about how you contribute to a DIY space instead of being a consumer. It is not about leaving the space as you found it, but adding to the space and the people in it. Donations are as much a part of this as participating in a DIY space.

DIY events are not cheap events you can save money on to spend somewhere else, they are events you choose to support because you support what the event and space stands for. You choose to give more to lessen the financial burden on the organizers and the space as a whole and create the financial opportunities needed to add even more to the space.

Facilitating costs

We aim to cover the costs for a facilitator who is facilitating during PlayHouse. This could be things like the basic snacks that are provided on the snack table during PlayHouse, materials that are needed for the workshop/skill-share that participants are not responsible for. And many other small costs. Facilitators may also choose to donate the costs as a donation to PlayHouse.

Politics

In participating in a DIY space and on the Nieuwland website, you can read more about the politics behind the PlayHouse donation guidelines.

Financially not able to donate?

Please let us know. We wish everyone to feel welcome and safe to attend regardless of financial means.

If you have any questions, please contact us by e-mail bigfatpervs@riseup.net
